ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'milk tea' class (FOODON_03301099); no specific entry for lychee-flavoured low-fat milk tea exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific lychee milk tea sub-code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for lychee milk tea as a food product.

serving.description: 1 medium cup (approx. 500 ml / 500 g as-served with ice)
serving.natural_language_portion: One medium-sized cup (approximately 500 ml) as typically served at KOI Thé and similar Taiwanese milk tea chains in Singapore, including ice.

nutrient_source.title: Milk tea, prepared with low-fat milk and sugar — estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference values for brewed black tea, low-fat milk, and lychee syrup components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for lychee-flavoured low-fat milk tea exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated by component modelling: brewed black tea (~60% by volume, USDA FDC #171917), low-fat milk 1% fat (~30% by volume, USDA FDC #172185), lychee syrup/flavouring (~10% by volume, estimated from USDA FDC lychee juice #168151 and generic sugar syrup). Standard KOI Thé medium cup is approximately 500 ml. Per-100 g values derived from weighted blend. Sugar content reflects standard sweetness level (100%) as served; actual sugar may vary with customer customisation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened milk tea estimated from analogous foods: sweetened tea-based beverages and low-fat milk; GI of sucrose-sweetened beverages approximately 55–65; low-fat milk GI approximately 27–34 (Atkinson et al., 2008)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lychee milk tea. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) low-fat milk GI ~27–34 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) sucrose and lychee syrup contribution raising blended GI toward low-medium range; (3) sweetened tea-based beverages with milk reported at GI ~50–60 in peer-reviewed literature. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 52.5 g carbohydrate in 500 g serving) / 100 = 28.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~100 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: A medium serving of low-fat lychee milk tea contains approximately 100 mg sodium, which is low and unlikely to significantly impact blood pressure; however, individuals with hypertension should monitor total daily sugar and caloric intake from sweetened beverages.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (~50 g per 500 ml serving at standard sweetness) and low glycemic index (~55)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this beverage due to its high free sugar load (~50 g per medium serving at 100% sweetness), which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation despite a low glycemic index of 55; requesting reduced or zero sugar customisation is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content contributing to excess caloric intake and potential dyslipidaemia
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sweetened milk tea beverages is associated with increased risk of dyslipidaemia and weight gain; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake and opt for reduced-sugar or unsweetened versions to minimise cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Low Fat Lychee Milk Tea (Medium) is a Taiwanese-style sweetened milk tea beverage sold at KOI Thé and similar bubble tea chains in Singapore. It is prepared by combining brewed black tea with low-fat milk and lychee-flavoured syrup, served chilled over ice in a medium-sized cup of approximately 500 ml. The drink has a light, floral-sweet lychee aroma with a creamy, mildly astringent tea base and a refreshing, lightly milky texture. The use of low-fat milk reduces the fat content compared to full-cream versions, but the beverage typically contains a substantial amount of added sugar from lychee syrup and sweetener at standard sweetness levels. Per 100 g, the drink provides approximately 52 kcal, 1 g protein, 0.4 g fat, and 10.5 g carbohydrate, predominantly as free sugars (~10 g per 100 g). A full medium serving (500 ml) delivers approximately 260 kcal and 50 g of free sugar at 100% sweetness, making it a high-sugar beverage. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low), and the glycemic load per serving is high at approximately 28.9. Clinically, this beverage is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its free sugar load, despite its low glycemic index, and of moderate concern for cardiovascular health due to its contribution to excess caloric and sugar intake. Sodium content is low (~100 mg per serving), making it less concerning for hypertension when consumed occasionally. The drink is vegetarian, halal-friendly, and gluten-free, but contains dairy (low-fat milk) and is not suitable for lactose-intolerant individuals or vegans. It is a popular afternoon or social beverage in Singapore's Taiwanese tea culture.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for lychee-flavoured low-fat milk tea exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Values are estimated by component modelling from USDA FoodData Central reference entries for brewed black tea, low-fat milk, and lychee juice/syrup. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for low-fat milk and sucrose-sweetened beverages. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central (brewed black tea FDC #171917, low-fat milk 1% fat FDC #172185, lychee juice FDC #168151) as no exact approved-source entry exists for this product. 2. Sugar content assumes standard 100% sweetness as served; KOI Thé allows customisation (0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100% sugar), which will significantly alter carbohydrate_g, energy_kcal, and glycemic load. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero by formulation — low-fat milk and tea base contain negligible PUFA at this dilution). trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero; low-fat milk and tea contain no trans fats). dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 (naturally zero — liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solids). 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources at the dilution modelled and is not a meaningful contributor in this beverage. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources. 5. GI set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for lychee milk tea was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. GI 55 is categorized as 'low' per University of Sydney GI Database standards. 6. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301099 and FoodEx2 code A0FKP are closest-parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for lychee-flavoured low-fat milk tea. 7. Multilingual names provided for Taiwanese cuisine served in Singapore context: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are included per Singaporean dish multilingual rules. 8. Serving size of 500 g used for glycemic load calculation (medium cup, approximately 500 ml, density ~1.0 g/ml). GL = (55 × 52.5) / 100 = 28.9.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full multilingual local_names for Taiwanese/Singapore context (Chinese simplified, traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Estimated per_100g nutrient values via USDA FoodData Central component modelling (brewed black tea, low-fat milk, lychee syrup) as no exact approved-source entry exists.
Documented all zero values (polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g) with justification phrases in notes.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit reasons in notes.

Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per University of Sydney GI Database standards (55 is considered low GI). Updated gi_source.source_match_notes, health_context.type2_diabetes.guidance, llm_training.natural_language_description, and notes to reflect this change.
Used closest_parent match_type for FoodOn and FoodEx2; none for SNOMED CT — all within permitted values.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent — all language-specific strings appear verbatim in flat aliases.
health_context includes Type 2 Diabetes with human_condition_name correctly set and high concern level with clinical guidance.
